text: This video demonstrates some steps during transanal total mesorectal excision for rectal carcinoma.
The procedure is done as sequential one-team approach, starting in the abdomen and followed by transanal part.

Following the completion of key abdominal steps, a light source is introduced through a ten-millimeter port located in the right iliac fossa, illuminating the surgical field for the transanal part of the procedure.
The light cable is wrapped around the rectum, and the abdomen is desufflated. The light remains in place and is switched on and off as needed during the procedure.
At the start of the transanal part of the procedure, the light source is visible shining through the rectal tissue.
The surgeon begins the transanal part of the procedure by closing the lumen using a purse string suture, securing the tissue around the rectal opening.
The light source is clearly visible throughout the transanal part of the procedure, illuminating the surgical field.
The surgeon continues the dissection with guidance from the light source, which illuminates the surgical field.
The surgeon performs the transanal dissection with enhanced visibility, utilizing transillumination from the light source positioned in the abdomen, which illuminates the tissue from within.
